arts.ttl: Bayern Munich to retire the #5 jersey in honour of Franz Beckenbauer
arts.sum: At their annual general meeting, Bayern Munich have announced that they will retire the number five jersey in honour of legendary defender Franz Beckenbauer. Beckenbauer died earlier this year due…

arts.ttl: Report: Five clubs battling to sign Bayern Munich’s Mathys Tel on loan
arts.sum: Stuttgart, [Werder] Bremen, Augsburg, Lazio and Valencia are among many clubs interested in signing Mathys Tel on loan. Just how many clubs want to sign Mathys Tel?

arts.ttl: Shakhtar Donetsk vs. Bayern Munich preview: Team news and predicted lineups
arts.sum: Here is our preview of Tuesday's Champions League clash between Shakhtar Donetsk and Bayern Munich, including our predicted lineups.

arts.ttl: Bavarian Podcast Works S7E20: Bayern Munich has another injury crisis; Talking Bayern’s number retirement; Why Bayern needs Florian Wirtz; and MORE!
arts.sum: Bayern Munich is down to its final three games before the Winterpause and some might consider the team to be a little bit wobbly at this point — albeit with a pretty sturdy Jamal Musiala-infused crutch to keep them upright (shout out, Toni Kroos). Another big week is on tap for Bayern Munich...

arts.ttl: Shakhtar Donetsk vs. Bayern Munich: UEFA Champions League probable teams, match stats and LIVE blog!
arts.sum: Shakhtar treated supporters at their adopted home ground in Lviv to a 3-1 win over Vorskla on Thursday, extending their unbeaten domestic run to 11 matches, but the Russo-Ukrainian War means they have been playing their 'home' matches in the Champions League at Schalke's Veltins-Arena - a stadium where Bayern haven't lost in 14 years. Bayern Munich are still without Harry Kane for their meeting with Ukrainian champions Shakhtar Donetsk in Gelsenkirchen on Tuesday (kick-off: 9pm CET).

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Liverpool could make move for Bayern Munich’s Leroy Sané; Jamal Musiala wouldn’t have changed a thing about his career journey so far; Federico Chiesa to Napoli?; Bayern confident on Alphonso Davies talks; and MORE!
arts.sum: Bayern Munich head coach Vincent Kompany clearly like Davies as a player, but is obviously not going to be handling the negotiations. Bayern Munich star Leroy Sané faces an uncertain future in Bavaria, but he should have a plethora of options should things not work out for him in Germany.

arts.ttl: Paul Wanner says Jamal Musiala and Florian Wirtz do not factor into Germany or Austria decision
arts.sum: Bayern Munich player Paul Wanner has been struggling to pick between representing Austria and Germany for some time now. But Wanner points to a recent press conference with Musiala to show that the former Chelsea FC youngster already gave him all the advice he needs: “When I joined the Bayern pros, he hadn’t been at the top for very long either.

arts.ttl: Inside Bayern Munich’s canceled pursuit of Hugo Ekitiké
arts.sum: When Robert Lewandowski departed Bayern Munich for FC Barcelona in the summer of 2022, it sent shockwaves through the footballing world — and left the Bavarians scrambling to replace him. The current Eintracht Frankfurt striker was once thought of as a potential replacement for Robert Lewandowski.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich reactions to the Club World Cup draw
arts.sum: The draw for the first ever revamped version of the FIFA Club World Cup has concluded and Bayern Munich were drawn into a group with Auckland City (not the same team that is currently top of the A-League Men), Benfica, and Boca Juniors (whom Bayern beat in the 2001 Intercontinental Cup in Japan). It’s a great honour to be able to compete with FC Bayern at this Club World Cup. I’m really looking forward to this new tournament, where we players can compete with the best in the world at club level.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Thomas Müller really went and tried that
arts.sum: In the 38th minute of Bayern Munich ’s 4-2 victory over FC Heidenheim on Saturday, Thomas Müller found himself with back to goal but in a good position to try the audacious. After Heidenheim equalized in the 51st minute, Müller was off for Jamal Musiala on what was supposed to be the younger superstar’s rest day.
